title: Add ability to prune containers and images
body:
Allow user to prune unused/unnamed images, the layer images from building,
via podman rmi --prune.

Allow user to prune stopped/exiuted containers via podman rm --prune.

This should resolve #1910

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mtrmac PTAL at the image prune logic
I'm a little iffy on the container prune thing (I don't think many people are actually going to use it), but I don't care that much - if we think people will use it, I won't argue.

I find `rmi -p` a bit confusing.  From the description I expected it to remove all images that are not used by any container.  I've tried it without any container and it didn't remove any image from the storage.
i initially had it that way and @rhatdan said he preferred it in rmi/rm.  I just do what I'm told ...
At the potential risk of @baude rath, I did not know that Docker had those commands, so I was wrong and @baude was correct originally.  We should follow what they have done.

^^ /me is currently framing ^^ 

no problem, ill switch it back
